meta_title: For Freelancers: client workflow to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Client Workflow to Solve No Design Skills
content.seo_description: Master a professional client workflow to overcome a lack of design skills. A high-impact case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for rapid development.
content.answer_block: Overcome design limitations by implementing a system-driven workflow. Use Antigravity to generate modular UI components and Claude Code to automate implementation. By treating design as code, you eliminate the need for manual Figma work, allowing you to ship professional, responsive SaaS interfaces at 10x the standard industry speed.
content.intro:
The client is staring at the screen, waiting for the prototype, and you are staring at a blank canvas, paralyzed by a lack of design intuition. In the freelance world, 'I'm not a designer' is a death sentence for your hourly rate. The gap between your backend competence and your frontend output is where your profit margins go to die.

But what if you didn't need to be a designer? By shifting from manual pixel-pushing to a programmatic design workflow, you can leverage AI to enforce consistency, rhythm, and aesthetics. This isn't about learning CSS grids—it's about building a production-ready engine that generates professional interfaces while you focus on the logic that actually pays the bills.

steps.title: Define the Design System Architecture
steps.description: Before writing code, establish a strict design constraint system (typography, spacing, color palette) to ensure AI output remains consistent across the entire application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Tailwind CSS configuration file that defines a professional, modern color palette (slate/indigo), a modular spacing scale, and a typography system using Inter. Ensure the output is a standard tailwind.config.js format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create tailwind.config.js with professional design tokens.'

steps.title: Generate Component Schemas
steps.description: Use Antigravity to convert high-level functional requirements into structured UI component schemas, ensuring your AI-generated code has a clear blueprint to follow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a list of essential SaaS UI components (Navbar, Hero, Feature Grid, Pricing Table) based on a 'minimalist enterprise' aesthetic. Provide the component structure as React functional components using Radix UI primitives.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the React components based on the schemas provided by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Automate Responsive Layout Logic
steps.description: Eliminate the guesswork of responsiveness by instructing your workflow to handle breakpoints programmatically rather than manually tweaking media queries.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a utility-first CSS layout wrapper that handles mobile-first responsiveness for a standard dashboard layout, including a collapsible sidebar and a main content area.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the responsive layout wrapper into the main App.tsx file.'

steps.title: Inject Dynamic Content Hooks
steps.description: Ensure your UI components are decoupled from static data. This makes them reusable for future clients, significantly reducing your long-term maintenance overhead.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the generated components to accept props for all text and image content. Create a mock data interface that simulates a real-world API response.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor all components to be data-driven with TypeScript interfaces.'

steps.title: Final Polish and Accessibility Audit
steps.description: Use Claude Code to conduct an automated accessibility scan and visual consistency check to ensure the final output meets professional agency standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase for common accessibility violations and visual inconsistencies. Suggest specific CSS adjustments to improve contrast and touch target sizes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run an accessibility audit and apply fixes to the UI components.'

pro_tips: Always define your design tokens (colors/spacing) in a single file; AI models perform better when constraints are explicitly stated upfront.
Treat your AI-generated components as 'drafts' and use Claude Code to perform iterative 'refinement passes' rather than trying to get the perfect output in one prompt.
Store your most successful Antigravity prompts in a local library; building a repository of 'design patterns' makes your next project 50% faster.
When facing design blocks, ask the AI to 'critique the current layout against Apple’s Human Interface Guidelines' to get actionable, professional feedback.
Focus on whitespace and alignment; if the AI-generated UI looks messy, reduce the number of elements per row rather than trying to fix the styling.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is live, begin creating a personal 'Component Library' using these generated assets to turn your freelance workflow into a scalable product business.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting with pixels and start shipping high-value SaaS products that command premium fees. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
